Pulled from the full job description

Full job description
We are looking for a Team Manager for a organisation's Fostering service in West Midlands. This is a full time and hybrid working position.

You must have a Diploma/Degree in Social Work and the right to work in the UK (no VISA sponsorship)

About the team

This organisation is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of Children and families with a child-centred approach. This team has flexible and creative ways of working and is well known for being a champion of equality and diversity within the Fostering community. This organisation specialises in working with children with disabilities.

About you

The successful candidate will have Senior Social Worker or Management experience within Fostering Social Work teams post qualification and whilst having an up-to-date understanding of relevant legislation. You will also need to be willing to travel into the office at least 2/3 days per week.

What's on offer?

A salary of up to £55,000 depenedent on experience
Mileage covered
Private medical insurance
Private dental insurance
On site parking
Various discounts
Training & development opportunities
Hours: Full time / Permanent

For more information, please contact Jamil Olweny

07587 031 098 / jolweny@charecruitment.com

What do you get from working with me?

CV enhancement
Application form enhancement
Access to many jobs in the market
One central point of contact
Interview preparation assistance
Expert consultation
Questions answered
Offer negotiation
shortCompanyReason: Recruitment advert posted by Charles Hunter Associates for a fostering team manager role.
shortRoleReason: Team Manager role for a Fostering service in the West Midlands.
companySummary: Charles Hunter Associates is recruiting for a fostering service role in the West Midlands.
roleSummary: Lead a fostering team in a hybrid, full-time permanent role supporting children and families, with a focus on safeguarding and children with disabilities.
headlineAttraction: Up to £55,000 salary with hybrid working and strong benefits.
rolePurpose: To manage a fostering social work team and support safe, child-centred fostering services.
travelRequired: Yes, office attendance at least 2/3 days per week
advertSummary: A West Midlands fostering service is seeking a Team Manager with social work qualifications and senior fostering experience. The role is full time, permanent and hybrid, with office attendance required 2-3 days per week. The service is child-centred, values equality and diversity, and specialises in working with children with disabilities.
personalisedIntro: I’m looking at this role because it sits at the point where leadership, service control and child-centred practice all matter at once. My background is not in fostering specifically, but I’ve led complex, regulated services where standards, people management and day-to-day delivery all had to hold together under pressure.
whyThisRole: This feels like a role for someone who can hold the team, the process and the safeguarding detail together without losing sight of the child. Based on the brief, I would be contributing to service stability, good oversight and consistent decision-making across a hybrid team.
companyPridePoints: Flexible and creative ways of working
coreResponsibilities: Support safeguarding and welfare of children and families
Travel to the office 2-3 days per week
essentialRequirements: Right to work in the UK
Senior Social Worker or management experience in fostering social work teams
senioritySignals: Senior Social Worker or Management experience
likelyBusinessNeeds: Experienced manager to maintain service quality
Coverage in a hybrid working model

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Operational leader who brings structure, safeguarding focus and calm team oversight.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’m looking at this role because it sits at the point where leadership, service control and child-centred practice all matter at once. My background is not in fostering specifically, but I’ve led complex, regulated services where standards, people management and day-to-day delivery all had to hold together under pressure.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Charles Hunter Associates is advertising a service that clearly values flexible working, equality and diversity, and a strong focus on children with disabilities. That combination matters to me because it suggests a team that wants both high standards and a practical, humane way of working.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This feels like a role for someone who can hold the team, the process and the safeguarding detail together without losing sight of the child. Based on the brief, I would be contributing to service stability, good oversight and consistent decision-making across a hybrid team.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Complex annual projects and team leadership
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Senior team leadership in a regulated environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve led and supported a team through business-critical work in a high-security, regulated setting, where service standards still had to be maintained. That gives me a strong base for managing people, workload and accountability.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the main leadership proof point.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve led a team through critical work without letting service standards slip.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Oversight, visibility and control across a service
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve built practical reporting logic to give managers a clearer view of pressure, bottlenecks and service strain. For a fostering team, I would bring that same habit of making the work visible and manageable.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as a supporting point for oversight and control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I like turning fragmented information into something a manager can actually use.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: Operational management and maintaining standards during change
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve worked in a scale-up environment where delivery discipline, resilience and people coordination all mattered. That experience is useful where a service needs steady management, not just good intent.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as evidence of calm operational control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’ve managed growth without losing grip on delivery.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can manage a team, keep safeguarding and legislation front of mind, and create enough structure for a hybrid service to run well. It also seems to need a leader who can work in a child-centred way, support colleagues, and stay steady in a service that values inclusion and consistency.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve managed workload, training and service standards in a team setting where deadlines and quality both mattered.
experienceMappings.relevance: This shows I can hold people, priorities and performance together.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in safety-critical and compliance-led environments where process discipline mattered and standards had to be clear.
experienceMappings.relevance: That translates well to a role where safeguarding and compliance are central.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve worked in a high-security, regulated environment where accuracy, process and accountability were non-negotiable.
experienceMappings.relevance: It supports the kind of careful, policy-aware judgement this role needs.

experienceMappings.roleNeed: Service oversight in a hybrid model
experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve built reporting and visibility tools to help managers see pressure points and act early.
experienceMappings.relevance: That is useful where a team is split between office and remote working.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve led operations through growth and change while keeping delivery disciplined and resilient.
experienceMappings.relevance: This matters where a fostering service needs steady leadership and consistent standards.

focusAreasToBring.title: Clear team oversight
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on making expectations, priorities and follow-through clear so the team can work consistently and confidently.

focusAreasToBring.title: Practical service control
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep an eye on workload, reporting and day-to-day pressure points so issues are dealt with early rather than left to build.

focusAreasToBring.title: Child-centred decision-making
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep the child’s welfare at the centre of decisions while still making sure the service runs in a disciplined way.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring senior operational leadership, regulated-environment experience and a track record of improving how services run. I’m used to combining people management, reporting and service control, which is why I think I can add value in a fostering team manager role.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend the first months getting close to the team, understanding how work is allocated and where pressure sits, and making sure oversight is clear. I’d also want to support consistent practice, strengthen reporting where needed, and help the service stay steady across a hybrid working pattern.
companyHighlights: Flexible and creative ways of working
Specialism in working with children with disabilities
Hybrid working with office attendance 2-3 days per week
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where standards matter, people are supported properly and the practical detail is taken seriously. The culture signals here suggest a team that values that balance, and that suits how I work.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get to know the team, the workflow, the key risks and the current reporting rhythm so I can understand where support is most needed.
I would look for simple ways to improve visibility around workload, priorities and follow-through, while staying close to safeguarding and practice standards.
I would focus on reinforcing team routines, supporting colleagues through any pressure points and making sure the service feels well held across hybrid working.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring steady leadership, practical control and a child-centred approach to a fostering team that needs all three.
closingProofPoints: I improved NHS patient review scores from 2.09 to 4.51.
I supported a team through business-critical work in a regulated environment.
I’ve led operations through growth while keeping delivery disciplined.
contentNotes: I have not claimed direct fostering experience because the CV evidence provided does not show it.
I have used adjacent regulated-service leadership evidence to support fit for safeguarding, oversight and team management.
Company facts are limited to the advert, so company-specific commentary is kept brief and factual.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
